commands.review_summary:
The PR changes model-call diagnostics to count high-frequency streamed text/thinking/tool-call deltas from incremental `delta` bytes, updates logging and OTEL metric wording, and adds focused regression tests plus two type-only cleanups.

PR surface: Source +31, Tests +101, Docs +2. Total +134 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the remaining metric behavior: current main strips `partial` snapshots but still serializes the snapshotless delta event, while the PR branch counts only incremental `delta` bytes. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is maintainer-labeled, dirty against current main, and changes an exposed diagnostics metric contract, so the next step is human rebase plus compatibility review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(agents): count streamed model deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary
- Count streamed `text_delta`/`thinking_delta`/`toolcall_delta` diagnostics from their incremental `delta` bytes instead of serializing the full event.
- Drop growing `partial` snapshots from fallback stream byte accounting so diagnostics do not repeatedly walk the accumulated assistant message.
- Add regression coverage proving text-delta accounting does not serialize large partial snapshots and keeps streams alive if byte inspection throws.
- Document the bounded `responseStreamBytes` semantics for high-frequency streamed deltas and align OTEL docs/metric descriptions with that bounded payload contract.

Refs #86599.

## Verification
- Rebasing onto current `origin/main` completed on head `7af7838e562`; obsolete unrelated gate-unblocker commits were dropped because their patch contents are already upstream.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed after rebase.
- `node scripts/docs-list.js` - passed.
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md docs/logging.md` - passed after rebase.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/logging.md extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts` - passed after rebase.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --threads=1` - passed after rebase.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts --reporter=dot` - passed after rebase, 2 shards / 86 tests.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean after rebase, no accepted/actionable findings, `overall: patch is correct (0.86)`.
- Previous AWS Crabbox `check:changed`, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4605e66e6f2f`, run `run_e94bc87a3cea`, base `b7e9272dbec`, head `013fb59dc0e`: exit 0. Post-rebase GitHub CI is the current remote gate for head `7af7838e562`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: dense model streams no longer make model-call diagnostics serialize the full growing `partial` assistant message on every delta.
Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree after rebase; earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate before the final rebase; GitHub CI is running on the rebased head.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md docs/logging.md`; `node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --threads=1`.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed, including assertions that `text_delta` accounting does not call `partial.toJSON`, counts incremental `delta` bytes, and keeps stream delivery alive when byte inspection cannot read a chunk; the OTEL service test suite still passes with the updated response-byte metric description; logging and OpenTelemetry docs now describe the same bounded metric semantics.
Observed result after fix: `responseStreamBytes` is computed from incremental delta bytes for high-frequency stream deltas and from snapshotless chunks for other partial-bearing stream events, while OTEL metadata and docs describe the exported histogram as bounded streamed response payload bytes.
What was not tested: a live long-running local-model session; the fix is validated through the diagnostics stream accounting path and changed-gate/CI proof.
